title: muscari neglectum var. atlanticum
intro: Muscari neglectum var. atlanticum, also known as the bluebell of Spain, is a flowering plant species in the asparagaceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, this plant is valued for its expectorant properties, which help relieve coughs and congestion due to its active compounds such as muscarine and saponins. The health benefits of Muscari neglectum var. atlanticum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as a natural antispasmodic, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusing the flowers or roots in hot water to make a tea, or using the plant in tincture form to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
